title: Ensure 'PHP version' is currently supported (if in use)

input_row.title: Ensure 'PHP version' is currently supported (if in use)
input_row.description: Periodically, older versions of PHP may be deprecated and no longer supported. Using a supported version of PHP for App Service deployment slots is recommended to avoid potential unpatched vulnerabilities.
input_row.rationale: Deprecated and unsupported versions of programming and scripting languages can present vulnerabilities which may not be addressed or may not be addressable.
input_row.audit: Take note of the currently supported versions of PHP here: https://www.php.net/supported-versions.php Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Configuration. 6. In the General settings pane, ensure that for a Stack of PHP, the Major version and Minor version reflect a currently supported release. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each app and deployment slot. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list apps: az webapp list For each app, run the following command to list deployment slots: az webapp deployment slot list --resource-group --name For each deployment slot, run the following command to get the PHP version: az resource show --name web --resource-group -- namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type config --parent sites//slots/ --query properties.[linuxFxVersion,phpVersion] If PHP is in use, ensure the version is currently supported. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: f466b2a6-823d-470d-8ea5-b031e72d79ae - Name: 'App Service app slots that use PHP should use a specified 'PHP version''
input_row.remediation: Note: No action is required if PHP is not in use. Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Configuration. 6. In the General settings pane, for a Stack of PHP, set the Major version and Minor version to a currently supported release. 7. Click Save. 8. Click Continue. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each app and deployment slot requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI Run the following command to list supported runtimes: az webapp list-runtimes For each deployment slot requiring remediation, run the following command with the appropriate parameters to update the PHP version: az resource update --name web --resource-group -- namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type config --parent sites//slots/ --set properties.[linuxFxVersion|phpVersion]=""
input_row.impact: If your app is written using version-dependent features or libraries, they may not be available on more recent versions. If you wish to update, research the impact thoroughly.
input_row.default_value: The version is selected during creation.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Log into the AWS Management Console.
2. Navigate to the Elastic Beanstalk service or wherever your PHP application is hosted (e.g., EC2 instances running PHP applications).
3. For Elastic Beanstalk: Select your application and environment where PHP is deployed.
4. For EC2: Connect to the EC2 instance via SSH.
5. Run the command `php -v` to check the PHP version installed on the server.
6. Compare the version reported with the list of currently supported PHP versions available at: https://www.php.net/supported-versions.php.
7. If the version is not a currently supported release, you will need to update it accordingly.
8. Detailed upgrading instructions can typically be found in the PHP documentation or through your application’s deployment process.

**What to verify/validate:**
- Confirm that the PHP version matches one of the supported versions listed on the PHP website.

**Expected secure configuration:**
- The PHP version running on your server must be one that is listed as currently supported by PHP.

**How to confirm compliance:**
- Ensure that the output from the `php -v` command shows a version number listed on https://www.php.net/supported-versions.php. If it does not, the PHP version is out-of-compliance and should be updated.
step2_review:
### APPROACH VALIDATION: PARTIALLY_AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:** The current classification of the audit approach as manual is partially appropriate. While the manual steps are valid, this check can be automated using AWS capabilities.
- **AWS APIs/boto3/CLI:** This compliance check can be automated using AWS Systems Manager (SSM) to execute commands on EC2 instances or Elastic Beanstalk environments. AWS SSM Run Command can be used to run the `php -v` command on instances. Additionally, AWS Config can be utilized to create a custom rule to check PHP versions if they are reported as part of the instance metadata or through a Lambda function.

### TECHNICAL ACCURACY: 6

- **Automation Details:** The assessment lacks automation details, which could significantly streamline the process. There is no mention of using AWS Systems Manager or AWS Config, which are suitable for such tasks.
- **Manual Steps:** The manual steps are technically correct but incomplete for a comprehensive audit approach. They should include guidance on using AWS services to automate the check.
- **Program Naming Convention:** The program name is not provided. A suggestion could be `aws_ec2_php_version_supported_check`.

### IMPROVEMENTS:

- **Automation:** Leverage AWS Systems Manager to automate the retrieval of PHP versions across instances. Use the `boto3` library to interact with AWS SSM and automate the execution of commands.
- **AWS Config:** Consider creating a custom AWS Config rule to evaluate PHP versions on EC2 instances.
- **Alternative Methods:** Use AWS Lambda in conjunction with AWS Config to periodically check PHP versions and ensure compliance.
- **Program Name Suggestion:** `aws_ec2_php_version_supported_check` or `aws_elasticbeanstalk_php_version_supported_check` for clarity and specificity.

### CONFIDENCE: MEDIUM

- The manual steps are accurate, but the lack of automation options reduces the overall effectiveness of the assessment. There is potential for significant improvement by incorporating AWS automation capabilities.

### FINAL RECOMMENDATION: MODIFY

- **Modify:** Incorporate AWS automation capabilities to improve efficiency and accuracy. Use AWS Systems Manager to automate the version check and AWS Config for continuous compliance monitoring. This will reduce manual effort and ensure consistent compliance checks across all environments.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** The task of checking PHP versions on AWS-hosted applications can be automated using AWS Systems Manager (SSM) to execute commands on EC2 instances or Elastic Beanstalk environments. This approach leverages AWS capabilities to streamline the process, ensuring consistency and efficiency across multiple instances. The second AI opinion correctly identifies the potential for automation using AWS services, which aligns with AWS best practices for compliance checks.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
aws_ssm_ec2_php_version_supported_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- AWS Resource/Service: EC2 Instances, Elastic Beanstalk Environments
- API/CLI Method: Use AWS Systems Manager (SSM) Run Command to execute `php -v` on instances
- Properties to Check: Output of the `php -v` command
- Pass Criteria: PHP version matches one of the supported versions listed on https://www.php.net/supported-versions.php
- Fail Criteria: PHP version does not match any of the supported versions, indicating non-compliance

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** The first AI opinion suggested a manual approach, while the second AI opinion highlighted the potential for automation. By evaluating AWS capabilities, it is clear that automation is feasible and preferable, resolving the disagreement in favor of the second AI's suggestion.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Microsoft_Azure_Compute_Services_Benchmark_v2_0_0
